title: What happens inside a language model?
oneSentenceDefinition: A language model repeatedly transforms a token representation, adding updates in a shared residual stream until the final vector supports a prediction.
fullDefinition: A sentence is like a relay race: each layer reads the current baton, computes a small update, and passes the baton on. The useful object is the changing vector, not a single neuron.
setconcaRelevance: What happens inside a language model? supplies one design decision for SetConCA.

title: Tokens, layers, and activations
oneSentenceDefinition: Tokens are discrete inputs; activations are the numerical states produced as those inputs pass through layers.
fullDefinition: Give the model the same word in two sentences: the token id is fixed, but its activation can change because context changes the computation.
setconcaRelevance: Tokens, layers, and activations supplies one design decision for SetConCA.

title: Representations, features, and concepts
oneSentenceDefinition: A representation is a vector state; a feature is a direction or function that detects a recurring property; a concept is the human-level hypothesis attached to that feature.
fullDefinition: A map can store the same city with coordinates, pixels, or roads. A representation is the encoding; a feature is a useful direction through that encoding.
setconcaRelevance: Representations, features, and concepts supplies one design decision for SetConCA.

title: Why neurons are not concepts
oneSentenceDefinition: A neuron coordinate can respond to several features when the model stores more features than dimensions: this is polysemanticity.
fullDefinition: One shelf can hold several rarely co-occurring objects. Looking at the shelf's occupancy does not tell you which object caused it without context.
setconcaRelevance: Why neurons are not concepts supplies one design decision for SetConCA.

title: What SetConCA is trying to improve
oneSentenceDefinition: SetConCA tests whether coordinating sparse codes across multiple views of the same underlying item improves stability, specificity, and causal usefulness.
fullDefinition: Instead of judging a feature from one photograph, compare several photographs of the same object and ask what survives the viewpoint change.
setconcaRelevance: What SetConCA is trying to improve supplies one design decision for SetConCA.
